content.claim: Since 2022, Hungary has become Europe's most socially selective school system according to PISA; Hungary's social selectivity index worsened by 5.3 points between 2018 and 2022, the largest deterioration in Europe alongside Poland's tracking re-introduction.
content.context: Early selection at age 10–12 into parallel 6- and 8-year grammar schools is the primary structural cause of Hungary's current position as Europe's most socially selective education system and should be phased out by 2035 in favor of single-entry-age (14 or 15) and unified secondary-school admission to reduce inequality of opportunity without sacrificing demonstrable value-added in pedagogy.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
